skill.name: plugin-settings
skill.description: Guide for storing per-project Claude Code plugin settings in .claude/*.local.md files with YAML frontmatter and Markdown notes.
skill.summary: Store and read local Claude Code plugin settings with Markdown and YAML frontmatter.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Official Anthropic skill reviewed with minimal audit scope. Static analysis flags filesystem and external command patterns because examples read local .claude settings files and use shell parsing, but no malicious behavior or prompt injection was found.

content.value_statement: Plugin behavior often needs project-specific settings without committing user preferences. This skill shows how to store and read local Markdown settings with YAML frontmatter.
actual_capabilities: Explains the .claude/plugin-name.local.md settings pattern for Claude Code plugins.
Provides templates for basic, advanced, agent state, and feature flag settings files.
Shows shell examples for reading YAML frontmatter and Markdown body content.
Demonstrates a command flow for asking users and creating settings files.
Includes validation, gitignore, permission, and restart guidance for local settings.
limitations: It does not install a runtime settings framework or package dependency.
Shell parsing examples support simple YAML frontmatter, not every YAML feature.
Claude Code must be restarted before hook settings changes take effect.
Plugin authors must adapt placeholder names, fields, and validation rules.

use_cases.title: Add User Settings to a Plugin
use_cases.description: Create a local settings file pattern so each project can control plugin behavior separately.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Agent State
use_cases.description: Store agent task details, coordinator session names, and project notes in a readable local file.

use_cases.title: Gate Hook Behavior by Configuration
use_cases.description: Read enabled flags and validation modes before a hook accepts, denies, or skips work.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Settings Template
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new plugin.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a basic local settings template for my Claude Code plugin. Include enabled state, mode, Markdown notes, gitignore guidance, and restart guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Add Settings to a Command
prompt_templates.scenario: Interactive command that creates project settings.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Claude Code command that asks users for plugin preferences, validates answers, and creates a project-local settings file.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Settings-Aware Hook
prompt_templates.scenario: Hook behavior controlled by local configuration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me update a Claude Code hook so it reads local plugin settings, validates fields, applies defaults, and handles disabled mode cleanly.

prompt_templates.title: Harden a Plugin Settings Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of security and maintainability.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my plugin settings workflow for path handling, input sanitization, default behavior, permissions, gitignore coverage, and clear user documentation.

output_examples.input: Create a project-local settings pattern for a validation plugin.
output: A recommended settings file name under the .claude directory.
A concise list of fields for enabled state, validation mode, and size limits.
User instructions covering local-only storage, gitignore rules, and restart requirements.

output_examples.input: Make my hook respect plugin settings.
output: A hook flow that exits when settings are missing or disabled.
A frontmatter parsing approach for simple fields.
Validation checks for booleans, numeric limits, sensitive paths, and default fallback values.

output_examples.input: Audit my plugin settings design before release.
output: A review of file naming, local storage, permissions, and input validation.
A list of missing documentation for users and maintainers.
Recommended safeguards for path fields, free text, and committed secrets.

best_practices: Use .claude/plugin-name.local.md and document that the file is user-local.
Validate every setting before using it in hooks, commands, or agents.
Provide defaults when settings are missing and clear restart guidance after edits.
anti_patterns: Do not commit local settings files that contain user preferences or project state.
Do not trust free-text settings without sanitizing quotes, paths, and expected values.
Do not rely on complex YAML features when using simple shell parsing.

faq.question: What settings file name does this skill recommend?
faq.answer: Use .claude/plugin-name.local.md in the project root, where plugin-name matches your plugin.

faq.question: Why use Markdown with YAML frontmatter?
faq.answer: YAML frontmatter stores structured settings, while the Markdown body stores notes, prompts, or task context.

faq.question: Should local plugin settings be committed?
faq.answer: No. Add .claude/*.local.md and .claude/*.local.json to gitignore because these files are user-local.

faq.question: Can hooks read these settings?
faq.answer: Yes. Hooks can check for the file, parse simple frontmatter fields, and skip work when disabled.

faq.question: Do changes apply immediately?
faq.answer: No. Claude Code must be restarted before hook settings changes are reliably loaded.

faq.question: Does this skill parse full YAML?
faq.answer: No. The included shell patterns are for simple frontmatter fields and require validation for production use.
